name: Sidamo

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Sidamo (sida1246).

notes: Skeleton. Sidamo (Sidaama), a Highland East Cushitic (Afroasiatic) language of Ethiopia written in a Latin orthography modeled on Oromo Qubee. Ejectives use dedicated letters: q=[kʼ], x=[tʼ], c=[tʃʼ], ph=[pʼ], while ch=[tʃ] is the plain affricate; dh is taken as the implosive [ɗ] on the Oromo model (grapheme not fully confirmed in sources). Doubled vowels mark phonemic length and doubled consonants mark gemination. Pitch accent/tone is not written and is left unencoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Sidamo (sida1246)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
